ev.headline: Global EV Sales Hit 4M in Q1 2026, but Growth is Uneven
ev.summary: Global EV sales reached 4 million units in Q1 2026, down 3% year-over-year. Europe surged with record March sales while China and North America saw declines amid shifting regional dynamics.

ev.headline: BMW iX Dead in US as Automaker Prepares Neue Klasse Launch
ev.summary: BMW is ending US allocations of the iX electric SUV to prepare for the next generation of electric vehicles with new technology, having served its purpose as a flagship showcase.

ev.headline: Nissan Unveils Long-Term Mobility Intelligence Vision Focused on AI EVs
ev.summary: Nissan revealed its AI-defined vehicle strategy with expanded electrification, combining AI-powered tech with hybrid and EV options as it streamlines its global lineup and targets market expansion.

ev.headline: EV Sales Jump in Asia as High Oil Prices Drive Market Shift
ev.summary: Electric vehicle sales surged in Southeast Asia as fuel price spikes from the Middle East conflict pushed buyers toward cheaper-to-operate EVs, with Chinese manufacturers seeing doubled exports in March.

ev.headline: Nissan Juke Electric Revealed as Brand's Latest EV Launch
ev.summary: Nissan unveiled the third-generation Juke as a fully electric vehicle for Europe, built on the CMF-EV platform and featuring vehicle-to-grid capabilities to compete in the growing electric crossover segment.

ev.headline: UK EV Tax Changes Create Obstacles for Mass Adoption
ev.summary: New Vehicle Excise Duty rates in the UK will see electric car owners pay higher taxes, marking a shift away from EV incentives toward normalizing them as price gaps with gas vehicles remain substantial.

ev.headline: Used EV Prices Drop 8.5% Year-Over-Year as Affordability Improves
ev.summary: Used EV average prices fell to $35,000 in February, down 8.5% year-over-year, making them increasingly competitive with gas vehicles as more lease returns hit the market.

ev.headline: Tesla Model S and X Plaid Get Final Limited Edition 'Signature Series'
ev.summary: Tesla is producing a final 350-vehicle 'Signature Series' run of the Plaid Model S and Model X before flagship programs shut down, featuring exclusive Garnet Red paint and gold accents.

ev.headline: Toyota bZ EV Surges to Third Best-Selling EV in US Q1 2026
ev.summary: Toyota's updated bZ electric SUV jumped 79% to reach 10,042 units sold in Q1 2026, becoming the third best-selling EV behind Tesla's Model Y and Model 3, bucking industry decline trends.

ev.headline: Mercedes-Benz Overhauled EQS Sedan Achieves 575-Mile Range
ev.summary: Mercedes unveiled a heavily updated EQS electric sedan with new 800-volt architecture, steer-by-wire technology, and up to 926 km WLTP range, representing the biggest upgrade since 2021 launch.

ev.headline: Sodium-Ion EV Battery Delivers 11-Minute Charging and 450 km Range
ev.summary: BAIC Group achieved a significant breakthrough in sodium-ion battery technology with energy density over 170 Wh/kg, offering a lower-cost alternative to lithium as mass production nears.

ev.headline: BYD's Chief Scientist Says Solid-State EV Batteries Hit Critical Stage
ev.summary: BYD reported solid-state EV batteries entering a critical commercialization phase with plans for limited batches in 2027 and mass production by 2030, though challenges remain in ion stability.

ev.headline: Solid-State EV Batteries Reach 800-Mile Range Reality
ev.summary: Chery's Rhino series solid-state battery achieves 600 Wh/kg energy density enabling 800-mile range potential, while Factorial Energy plans to bring technology to market by 2027 with major OEM partnerships.

ev.headline: Slate Auto Closes $650M Series C to Bring EV Pickup to Production
ev.summary: Bezos-backed EV startup Slate Auto raised $650 million Series C funding, providing capital to push its affordable pickup truck into production before year-end 2026 at its Indiana facility.

ev.headline: Kia Plans US-Bound Pickup and Doubles Hybrid Models by 2030
ev.summary: Kia confirmed a body-on-frame pickup with hybrid and range-extended EV versions for the US, aiming for 1 million annual sales and 6.2% market share by 2030 with doubled hybrid lineup.

ev.headline: Only 6 Auto Brands Had EV Sales Growth in Q1 2026
ev.summary: Out of dozens of automakers, only Toyota, Lexus, Cadillac, Rivian, Lucid, and Ram achieved year-over-year EV sales growth in Q1 2026, while most brands saw declines of 60-90%.

ev.headline: Volvo Launches First Electric Articulated Hauler Trucks in Serial Production
ev.summary: Volvo began regular production of its A30 and A40 Electric articulated haul trucks, marking the first time electric articulated haulers of this size moved from pilot stage into serial production.

ev.headline: Washington State Expands Direct EV Sales to Rivian, Lucid
ev.summary: Bipartisan legislation in Washington allows more EV manufacturers including Rivian and Lucid to hold dealer licenses for direct sales, estimated to increase EV adoption by 13% by 2030.

ev.headline: Federal Government Proposes 100% US-Made EV Charger Requirement
ev.summary: Trump administration moves to restrict federal EV charger funding to only 100% US-made components, up from 55% requirement, aiming to boost domestic manufacturing despite potential supply challenges.

ev.headline: California Launches $200M EV Incentive to Counter Federal Tax Credit Loss
ev.summary: California announced a $200 million EV incentive program designed to offset the loss of the $7,500 federal tax credit and maintain pressure on automakers to electrify their fleets.

ev.headline: Automakers Announce $60B in EV Investment Writedowns
ev.summary: Major automakers including Stellantis, Ford, Honda, and GM announced collectively over $60 billion in financial writedowns related to scaling back overly aggressive EV transition strategies.

ev.headline: 32 New EV Models Launching in US in 2026 Despite Market Slowdown
ev.summary: Automakers are launching 32 new electric vehicle models in the United States this year despite weak sales, marking the beginning of a mature EV market with greater choice across price points.

ev.headline: New EV Sales Drop 28% in Q1 2026 Despite Used EV Surge
ev.summary: New EV sales cratered 28% year-over-year in Q1 2026 to 212,600 units, but used EV sales surged 12% with prices now within $1,300 of gas vehicles, signaling market reset after tax credit expiration.

ev.headline: Leapmotor Leads EV Startups with 50K Deliveries in March 2026
ev.summary: Chinese EV startup Leapmotor posted 50,029 vehicle deliveries in March 2026, up 34.87% year-over-year, with four new models launching in 2026 expected to account for 60% of annual sales.

energy.headline: Homeowner reviews Tesla off-grid tech claiming decades of utility bill savings
energy.summary: A homeowner shared their experience installing 30 solar panels and two Tesla Powerwalls, noting their system should pay for itself in 6-8 years with continued savings for decades as solar panels last 20+ years.

energy.headline: Backup power is the LEAST interesting thing your home battery can do
energy.summary: Home battery systems paired with solar and EVs offer energy optimization and cost arbitrage benefits beyond backup power, allowing homeowners to shift consumption from peak to low-price periods.

energy.headline: Anker Solix launches 7 kWh plug-in battery for rooftop solar retrofits
energy.summary: Anker Solix introduced the Solarbank Max AC, a 7 kWh modular home battery system designed to retrofit existing rooftop solar without full inverter replacement, expandable to 42 kWh with plug-in functionality.

energy.headline: Solar installs surged 205% before tax credit deadline, reshaping home electrification market
energy.summary: US homeowners rushed to install solar before December 2025 deadline, driving 205% surge in demand and pushing integrated home energy systems combining solar, storage, and EV charging as the new market norm.

energy.headline: Batteries become the new home solar as net metering evolves and energy prices soar
energy.summary: As net metering policies change like California's NEM 3.0, homeowners are increasingly choosing battery storage over grid exports, making home battery systems the centerpiece of residential energy systems alongside solar.

energy.headline: Colorado clearing way for portable plug-in solar and meter collar technology
energy.summary: Colorado is joining dozens of states allowing DIY plug-in solar panels and meter collars that cost $500-$700 versus $10,000 electrical panel upgrades, with systems available for ~$2,000 including battery storage.

energy.headline: Most US households can save money and weather blackouts with solar plus storage
energy.summary: Stanford research finds about 60% of families could reduce electricity costs by 15% on average with solar-battery systems, with benefits varying by state and electricity costs.

energy.headline: New Jersey leads solar incentives in 2026 with $85-90 per megawatt-hour locked rates
energy.summary: New Jersey's ADI program locks in roughly $90/MWh for 15 years combined with sales and property tax exemptions, making it the strongest state incentive package for residential solar in 2026.

energy.headline: California residents increasingly pairing battery storage with rooftop solar installations
energy.summary: Solar paired with battery installations now makes up about 9% of California's residential net metering capacity, with over 40,000 new installations added between October 2023 and April 2024.

energy.headline: Energy storage reaches record capacity amid 51% residential growth in 2025
energy.summary: US residential battery capacity grew 51% in 2025 and is expected to grow 120% by 2030, with home battery systems forming virtual power plants that enhance grid reliability while lowering costs for all ratepayers.

energy.headline: US enters post-incentive era for residential solar after ITC expiry surge
energy.summary: The 205% surge in solar installations before the December 31, 2025 deadline marked a major market shift, with third-party ownership models like leases and PPAs remaining eligible for incentives through 2028.

energy.headline: Colorado net metering stays strong as rates rise, boosting solar savings in 2026
energy.summary: Colorado law requires full retail-rate credits for exported solar power with 10% rate increases planned for 2026, making net metering more valuable yearly despite the loss of federal tax credits.

energy.headline: Arizona solar incentives shift away from net metering to utility net billing framework
energy.summary: Arizona utilities including APS and SRP now use net billing where excess solar energy earns credits at utility-set rates rather than full retail rates, encouraging battery pairing with solar systems.

energy.headline: Six states advance DIY plug-in solar legislation as energy affordability concerns mount
energy.summary: Massachusetts, Colorado, Maine and other states are rapidly advancing legislation allowing residential DIY plug-in solar panels and meter collars to reduce installation costs and barriers.

energy.headline: California battery attachment reaches 69% on rooftop solar systems amid NEM 3.0 transition
energy.summary: As California transitions from 1:1 net metering to Net Billing, residential battery attachment rates have surged to 69%, with emerging markets like Colorado potentially unlocking 2 GWh of new capacity by 2040.

energy.headline: Lithium-ion dominates residential storage market with 68% share in 2026
energy.summary: Lithium-ion batteries account for 68.1% of residential storage market revenue in 2026 due to superior energy density, longer cycle life, and higher efficiency compared to alternative technologies.

energy.headline: US battery storage expected to grow 21% in 2026 despite policy headwinds
energy.summary: Battery storage growth remains robust driven by energy arbitrage economics—charging at low prices and selling at peak times—despite tariffs and shifting renewable energy policies.

energy.headline: New York offers 25% state solar tax credit with property tax exemptions through 2035
energy.summary: New York provides a 25% state tax credit (up to $5,000) plus full net metering, sales tax exemption, and 15-year property tax exemption, creating one of the strongest incentive packages nationally.

energy.headline: US solar generation projected to grow from 290 to 420 TWh by end of 2026
energy.summary: With 24.3 GW of new battery storage coming online in 2026, EIA projects solar generation will surge 45% year-over-year as distributed small-scale solar grows 11% and utilities scale renewable deployments.

energy.headline: Global residential solar storage market projected to reach $462 billion by 2036
energy.summary: Residential solar energy storage market is projected to grow at 18.3% CAGR from 2026 to 2036, reaching $462.34 billion as lithium-ion technology dominates with superior performance and declining costs.

energy.headline: Virtual power plants prove capability with 100,000 California homes dispatching 535 MW to grid
energy.summary: Sunrun's virtual power plant test in July 2025 had over 100,000 California homes collectively dispatch 535 megawatts, demonstrating the potential of distributed residential solar and storage to meet grid peak demands.

energy.headline: Battery storage costs drive residential adoption as economics improve across markets
energy.summary: Net metering policy changes and rising electricity prices are making battery storage economically viable for more homeowners, with 6-10 kW systems offering optimal cost-to-capacity balance in 2026.

energy.headline: Solar manufacturing boom continues with 15.1 GW added capacity in 2025
energy.summary: US domestic solar manufacturing surged in 2025 with 15.1 GW of module capacity added, placing the US third globally for solar manufacturing and enabling full supply chain control from raw materials to recycling.

energy.headline: Bluetti launches modular 13K energy storage system for homes and small businesses
energy.summary: Bluetti's new EnergyPro 13K modular residential storage system starts at $7,919 with expandable capacity, offering flexibility for various home electrification needs and energy management scenarios.

energy.headline: US solar sector remains top new power source for fifth consecutive year at 43 GW
energy.summary: US installed 43.1 GW of solar capacity in 2025 despite 14% decline from 2024, remaining the dominant source of new power as residential sector ended virtually flat and community solar declined 25%.

v2x.headline: Rivian and Redwood Materials Announce Energy Storage Partnership
v2x.summary: Rivian and Redwood Materials partner to repurpose EV batteries into stationary energy storage systems, providing grid flexibility and reducing peak demand strain at Rivian's manufacturing facility.

v2x.headline: 2026 V2G Predictions: School Buses and Commercial Standards to Drive Bidirectional Charging Scale
v2x.summary: Expert predictions indicate 2026 will see electric school buses form the backbone of V2G capacity, with the first commercial V2G-AC offerings emerging for light-duty vehicles under UL certification.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging in 2026: Breakthrough Year for V2H and V2G Mass Adoption
v2x.summary: 2026 marks a turning point as bidirectional charging technology reaches mainstream adoption with EcoFlow and EcoFlow reporting major EV models now supporting V2L, V2H, and V2G capabilities.

v2x.headline: Enphase Energy Unveils IQ Bidirectional EV Charger for V2H and V2G
v2x.summary: Enphase Energy announced its new IQ Bidirectional EV Charger architecture enabling vehicle-to-home and vehicle-to-grid capabilities with AI-powered energy management, expected to launch in H2 2026.

v2x.headline: Massachusetts Launches Statewide V2X Pilot Program with 100 Bidirectional Chargers
v2x.summary: Massachusetts is deploying 100 bidirectional chargers to homes, schools, and towns through a MassCEC-backed two-year pilot converting EVs into grid assets for the state.

v2x.headline: Volvo and Polestar Launch Bidirectional V2H Charging in US with Dcbel Systems
v2x.summary: Swedish brands Volvo and Polestar now offer bidirectional vehicle-to-home charging in the United States using Dcbel's Ara Home Energy Station for select models.

v2x.headline: Mercedes-Benz Announces Bidirectional Charging for 2026 with MB.CHARGE Home System
v2x.summary: Mercedes-Benz revealed plans to introduce bidirectional charging in 2026 starting with the all-electric GLC, connecting vehicle, wallbox, and energy management through its MB.CHARGE Home system.

v2x.headline: UC Irvine Demonstrates Mass-Market V2H Technology with Kia EV9 and Wallbox Quasar 2
v2x.summary: Six homes in Menifee, California have been deployed with commercial V2H systems using Kia EV9 vehicles and Wallbox Quasar 2 chargers for a two-year real-world evaluation.

v2x.headline: Research Shows V2H Charging Can Save $2,400-$5,600 Over EV Vehicle Lifetime
v2x.summary: A new study finds that vehicle-to-home charging can reduce household electricity costs by 40-90% and cut building emissions by 70-250% over a 15-year vehicle lifetime.

v2x.headline: Utilities Scale Managed Charging Programs as EV Loads Approach 7.2 Million Vehicles
v2x.summary: US utilities are partnering with software providers and automakers to deploy managed charging programs that spread EV load and avoid peak demand peaks while deferring infrastructure upgrades.

v2x.headline: Retired EV Batteries Bolster Texas Grid with Second-Life Storage
v2x.summary: B2U Storage Solutions and Element Energy are repurposing end-of-life EV batteries to provide grid storage in Texas, with 500 GM batteries now serving as a flexible resource for ERCOT.

v2x.headline: US Achieves Self-Sufficiency in Grid Battery Manufacturing for First Time
v2x.summary: The United States can now meet 100% of domestic energy storage project demand with American-built batteries as grid storage becomes critical for data center expansion.

v2x.headline: EVs Could Meet Global Short-Term Grid Storage Demand by 2030
v2x.summary: Research published in Nature Communications shows EV batteries could satisfy global short-term storage needs by 2030 with just 12-43% participation in vehicle-to-grid programs.

v2x.headline: Nissan Leaf Supports V2H Charging in New NACS Models
v2x.summary: The redesigned Nissan Leaf now offers vehicle-to-home functionality through compatible energy management systems, enabling homeowners to use stored battery energy for peak shaving and outage backup.

v2x.headline: Which EV Models Support V2H: 2026 Guide from Recharged
v2x.summary: Updated 2026 guide shows growing roster of EV models with vehicle-to-home capability including Ford F-150 Lightning, GM Ultium vehicles, Tesla Cybertruck, and Kia/Hyundai models.

v2x.headline: Battery Storage Integration in EV Charging Stations Increases Revenue and Reduces Grid Impact
v2x.summary: Analysis shows on-site battery storage at EV charging stations can double net present value and reduce grid connection requirements by up to 80% while increasing economic viability.

v2x.headline: Advanced Battery Technologies for Smart Grids Achieved in 2025
v2x.summary: High-energy lithium-ion systems, quasi-solid-state configurations, and sodium-ion batteries advanced significantly in 2025 to meet smart grid integration needs with enhanced energy density and safety.

v2x.headline: V2H Battery Backup: Ford F-150 Lightning Powers Homes for Three Days During Outages
v2x.summary: Ford's F-150 Lightning provides up to three days of home backup power using its large battery pack through the Ford Charge Station Pro and Home Integration System.

v2x.headline: V2H and V2G Technology Potential Explores in Germany's BDL Next Project
v2x.summary: Germany's BDL Next project demonstrates how EV flexibility integrates into energy systems through bidirectional vehicles and wallboxes, with gridX managing the smart energy management architecture.

v2x.headline: Honda Announces Acura RSX with Bidirectional V2H and V2G Support (Planned)
v2x.summary: Honda announced plans for its upcoming Acura RSX to feature bidirectional home charging, previewing a future where all Honda EVs on the new global platform will support V2H and V2G.

v2x.headline: Department of Energy Releases Bidirectional Charging and EV Mobile Storage Guide
v2x.summary: DOE outlines how bidirectional EVs provide mobile battery storage for buildings and the grid through V2B and V2G applications, offering resilience, demand response, and cost reduction benefits.

v2x.headline: Wallbox Quasar 2 V2H Charger Powers Homes with CCS1 EV Compatibility
v2x.summary: Wallbox Quasar 2 bidirectional charger enables V2H and V2G with CCS1 vehicles, providing up to 11.5kW discharge power for home backup and grid services with managed schedules.
